title: Agent secret-read hard block
outcome: no_secret_read made enforceable, not advisory. Policy gains a [secrets] block_tools=["pass","age","sops"] declaration (manifest agent_safe=false remains the source of truth). lib/common.sh adds panoply_require_secret_access (mirrors panoply_require_human; exit 13 under PANOPLY_AGENT=1). panoply-doctor gains a read-only secrets-rail assertion phase: it verifies each policy-blocked tool is agent_safe=false in the manifest, the policy gate is true, and a live guard self-test blocks under PANOPLY_AGENT=1; it exits non-zero only on genuine drift. The consolidated gate validate-secrets.sh (moon: panoply:validate-secrets) was authored here and proves the block; agent-rails.md documents the hard block.
validation: panoply doctor exits 0 with 'secrets rail: ENFORCED' in both human and PANOPLY_AGENT=1 mode. Guard self-test: PANOPLY_AGENT=1 panoply_require_secret_access -> exit 13; human (PANOPLY_AGENT unset) -> exit 0. validate-secrets.sh [2/5] PASS. shellcheck --severity=warning clean (0 warnings/errors; only repo-baseline info-level SC2015/SC1091).
decisions: Chose the guard-helper + doctor self-test approach (no new command surface), per the epic's 'no new mechanism; codify in policy and assert in panoply doctor'
Rail check targets the policy block_tools list, not the whole secrets module, so the agent-safe scanner gitleaks (added in S4) is correctly excluded from the agent_safe=false requirement
doctor never invokes pass/age/sops; it tests only the guard function, so the assertion stays read-only and agent-safe
token_note: loaded 1 story block + 1 canon ref + STATE/ledger tail; compact PASS/FAIL + exit-code gate output
